title: Greenberg–Hastings cellular automaton
body: The Greenberg–Hastings Cellular Automaton (abbrev. GH model) is a two dimensional cellular automaton (abbrev. CA) named after James M. Greenberg and Stuart Hastings, designed to model excitable media. One advantage of a CA model is ease of computation. The model can be understood quite well using simple "hand" calculations, not involving a computer. Another advantage is that, at least in the three state case, one can prove a theorem characterizing those initial conditions which lead to repetitive behavior.
